name: First Bank
description_short: First Bank Ketchikan, AK — First Bank is an Alaska-based FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, mortgages, and consumer loa...
description_long:
First Bank operates as a traditional community bank headquartered in Alaska, serving customers across Southeast Alaska locations including Ketchikan, Juneau, Sitka, Petersburg, Wrangell, and Craig. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection up to regulatory limits.

First Bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including multiple checking account tiers (Income Checking, Value Checking, Anytime Checking, Student Anytime Checking), savings products (First Premium Savings, Money Market Accounts, Peak Savings, Time Deposits), and Individual Retirement Accounts. Their lending portfolio spans mortgage loans (including construction loans, home equity products, and accessory dwelling units), personal loans, auto loans, boat loans, RV loans, and land loans. The business segment includes business checking accounts at multiple service levels, business savings products, business loans, SEP IRAs, and merchant card services.

First Bank distinguishes itself through local community presence with physical branches in multiple Southeast Alaska towns, specialized lending products relevant to regional needs (commercial fishing loans, boat construction loans, leased land loans), and a stated commitment to community involvement including community development grants and charitable giving. They offer digital banking through online platforms, mobile banking, Zelle payments, and telephone banking alongside traditional branch services.

As a regional community bank, First Bank serves customers who prefer local relationship banking and have ties to Southeast Alaska. The primary limitation is geographic—the bank operates only in specific Alaska locations and may not serve customers in other states. Their product offerings are standard for regional banks, without distinctive premium features or rates that would set them apart from larger national competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Mortgage loans including construction and home equity products
Personal loans and personal lines of credit
Auto loans, boat loans, RV loans, and land loans
Business loans including commercial real estate and equipment financing
Online and mobile banking with bill pay
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full government backing
Multiple checking account options designed for different customer needs (student, income-based, value-focused)
Specialized loan products for Alaska residents including commercial fishing loans and boat construction loans
Accessible digital banking through online platform, mobile banking, and Zelle payments
Community involvement programs including development grants and charitable giving
Local branch presence across six Southeast Alaska communities
Comprehensive mortgage products including home construction and accessory dwelling unit loans
cons: Limited geographic availability—serves only Southeast Alaska locations, not accessible to out-of-state customers
No indication of competitive rates or rate comparisons with national banks
Limited information about fees, minimum balances, or account requirements on primary website
best_for: Alaska residents in Southeast communities seeking local relationship banking
Small business owners and commercial fishing operations in Alaska service areas
Homebuyers in Alaska needing specialized mortgage products like construction and ADU loans
Customers preferring physical branch banking combined with digital access
diagnosis: First Bank is best for Alaska residents in Southeast communities who value local relationship banking and need standard consumer or business banking products. The critical caveat is geographic limitation—this bank only serves specific Alaska towns and is not accessible to customers in other states or regions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
